mnemonics: SNAP: Storage, Network, Architecture, Processing - the four key hardware components to SNAP into place for a data warehouse.
keyPoints: Scalable server architecture (like MPP) is essential for handling large data volumes.
High-speed networks (like 10 GigE) are crucial to prevent data transfer bottlenecks.
I/O subsystem performance (using RAID configurations) is often the limiting factor for query speed.
commonMistakes: Underestimating network bandwidth requirements, leading to ETL bottlenecks.
Neglecting storage I/O optimization, causing slow query performance despite sufficient CPU.
Using a single-server architecture (SMP) for a large data warehouse instead of a scalable MPP architecture.
